source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/11_Audit F-up/240415-17 ISO 갱신심사 & 2,3G MDR 심사 - 3EC/MDR 심사/심사 대비 자료_240415-17/1. 기술문서 (A-TCF-5-149 TCF_24.04.12)/A-TCF-5-155 Risk Management/A-TCF-5-104 Risk Management Report (rev.4)_update.docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: Risk management report for the A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector models.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Documents risk management activities, risk evaluation, risk control, residual risk acceptability, and production/post-production information for the device.
keyPoints: The document identifies applicable EU and Korean regulations and relevant medical device, electrical safety, usability, software, labeling, radio, and EMC standards.
The device is classified as IIb and has an expected service life of approximately 5 years.
Before risk control, 43 risks were identified, with 2 acceptable and 41 unacceptable.
After risk control, all 43 identified residual risks were evaluated as acceptable.
Production and post-production information is collected and reviewed by Quality Assurance, R&D, Production, Management, and the Quality manager.

entities.name: H&abyz Co., Ltd.
entities.evidence: This is a document for the H&abyz Co., Ltd. to establish and document a risk management plan in accordance with the risk management procedure.

entities.name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector
entities.evidence: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

entities.name: European Union
entities.evidence: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017

entities.name: Republic of Korea
entities.evidence: Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

concepts.name: Risk management
concepts.evidence: systematic application of management policies, procedures (3.13) and practices to the tasks of analysing, evaluating, controlling and monitoring risk

concepts.name: Risk management file
concepts.evidence: risk management file set of records (3.16) and other documents that are produced by risk management

concepts.name: Risk acceptability criteria
concepts.evidence: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

concepts.name: Residual risk evaluation
concepts.evidence: After performing risk control measures, evaluate and record residual risks according to the criteria defined in the risk management plan.

concepts.name: Production and post-production information
concepts.evidence: activities related to collection and review of relevant production and post-production information.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: Manufacturer shall identify which functions are Essential Performance.

concepts.name: Reasonably foreseeable misuse
concepts.evidence: Might be misused by maintenance and calibration error of operator.

concepts.name: Benefit-risk analysis
concepts.evidence: The benefit-risk analysis is used to determine if the residual risk is outweighed by the expected benefits of the intended use of the medical device.

evidence.claim: The report covers the A1717MCW, A1417MCW, and F1417MCW models.
evidence.evidenceText: for A1717MCW / A1417MCW / F1417MCW

evidence.claim: The product is a Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector.
evidence.evidenceText: Product name: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector

evidence.claim: The document revision is Rev.4 dated April 12, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: 4 Apr/12/2024 Response to final review by 3EC (NB)

evidence.claim: The document was prepared, reviewed, and approved on April 12, 2024.
evidence.evidenceText: Prepared by R&D team Kim, Kihyun April 12, 2024 Reviewed by R&D Manager Lee, Dong jin April 12, 2024 Approved by Quality Manager Hur Namkyu April 12, 2024

evidence.claim: Applicable regulations listed include Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and the Medical Device Act in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 5 April 2017 Republic of Korea Medical Device Act in Korea

evidence.claim: The device classification is IIb.
evidence.evidenceText: Classification IIb
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector classification

evidence.claim: The expected service life is approximately 5 years.
evidence.evidenceText: Expected service life Approximately 5 years
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector service life

evidence.claim: The intended purpose is general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy for adults and children.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW is indicated for digital imaging solution designed for providing general radiographic diagnosis of human anatomy targeting both adult and children.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector intended purpose

evidence.claim: The device is not to be used for mammography, infants, pregnant women, fluoroscopy, or angiography.
evidence.evidenceText: Not to be used for mammography. Not to be used for infant and pregnant women. Not to be used for fluoroscopy and angiography.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector contraindications

evidence.claim: The device can be wired or wireless and supports auto exposure detection without generator integration.
evidence.evidenceText: The A1717MCW/A1417MCW/F1417MCW are wired or wireless digital flat panel detectors... The detectors support an auto exposure detection technology that allows the detectors to be used without generator integration.
evidence.pageTarget: Digital Flat Panel X-ray Detector technical description

evidence.claim: The risk analysis process includes intended use, safety characteristics, hazards and hazardous situations, and risk estimation.
evidence.evidenceText: The risk analysis process consists of the following steps... description of the intended use... identification of the characteristics... identification of hazards and hazardous situations... estimation of risks

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 43 risks were identified and estimated.
evidence.evidenceText: Through the identification of known and foreseeable hazards, 43 risks were identified and estimated.

evidence.claim: Before risk control, 2 risks were acceptable and 41 risks were unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable region: 2 ea Unacceptable region: 41 ea

evidence.claim: Risk levels 1 through 4 are acceptable and risk levels 5 through 25 are unacceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: Acceptable Risk (A): Acceptable Region, Green Zone, Risk levels: 1~4 Unacceptable Risk (U): Unacceptable Region, Red Zone, Risk levels: 5~25

evidence.claim: After risk control, all 43 residual risks were in the acceptable region.
The report states all identified residual risks after risk control were acceptable.
evidence.evidenceText: All identified residual risks evaluated after implementation of the risk control measures, are distributed in an acceptable region.

evidence.claim: Risk control measures included labeling, user manual, certified battery, IEC 60601-1 testing, differentiated cable types, IEC 62304 software development process, radio-related testing, and certified RF module.
evidence.evidenceText: As control measures for the identified risks, providing label and user manual, using certified battery, testing the device to meet IEC 60601-1, designing each cable type differently, establishing software development process in accordance with IEC 62304, radio-related testing, and using certified RF module, etc. were implemented and verified.

evidence.claim: Overall residual risks were determined to be acceptable through warning, operating method, adverse event reporting, and usability expert review.
evidence.evidenceText: Finally, the overall residual risks are determined to be acceptable through the warning, operating method and adverse event reporting and usability expert review.

evidence.claim: Production and post-production information is to be obtained through A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance.
evidence.evidenceText: Refer to A-TCF-5-161 Post-Market Surveillance for getting the information of production and post- production phases.

evidence.claim: Quality Assurance activities include customer satisfaction survey, complaint statistics, inspection monitoring, and measuring device monitoring.
evidence.evidenceText: Quality Assurance 1. Customer satisfaction survey... 2. Statistical processing of customer complaints... 3. Monitoring results of the incoming inspection and end product test results... 4. Monitoring the measuring devices

evidence.claim: The Quality manager is responsible for collecting, reviewing, and managing quality problems in production and post-production.
evidence.evidenceText: Responsibility and the role of the Quality manager Collect, review the information and manage the quality problems in production and post-production phase

evidence.claim: Risk analysis for collected production and post-production information is performed once every 12 months.
evidence.evidenceText: Risk analysis for the collected information (once every 12 months)

evidence.claim: The Quality manager shall report safety information and side effects to the regulatory agency when necessary.
evidence.evidenceText: According to the seriousness of the information collected, the Quality manager shall report safety information and any side effects to the regulatory agency (Applicable national or regional regulations).

reviewFlags.reason: Authority metadata is ambiguous: source metadata signals MFDS/domestic, but the source text lists both European Union Regulation (EU) 2017/745 and Republic of Korea Medical Device Act, and revision history references 3EC (NB).
Potential OCR or transcription quality issues appear in the text, including misspellings and malformed phrases such as 'corraective', 'dose not be sterilized', and 'know and foreseeable risks'.
Possible conflicting or erroneous standard references: the source lists EN 15223-1:2021 in normative references but later refers to EN 15322-1 and 15322-1 in review results.
